summary: First iteration test. Backend: 18/18 pytest tests passed end-to-end (auth, rooms, participants, deck, swipes+mutual match, matches enrichment, messages, reports, stats, close room). Frontend (mobile viewport 390x844): landing, host login, dashboard, create-room, room detail (QR + 6-char code + stats + Encerrar sala), reports tab, user join (with invalid code error + valid code -> profile) all render and function correctly. Connected to backend successfully.

issues: RN web warning: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated. Use 'boxShadow'. Non-blocking but noisy in console.

action_items: IMPORTANT - Agent-to-agent note from main agent referenced incorrect frontend testIDs (e.g. 'enter-as-user-btn', 'host-login-btn', 'host-email-input', 'room-name-input', 'room-create-submit', 'room-qr', 'room-stats', 'reports-empty'). Actual testIDs in code are different: 'cta-user', 'cta-host', 'email-input', 'password-input', 'login-btn', 'name-input', 'desc-input', 'create-submit', 'room-code', 'tab-qr', 'tab-reports', 'close-room-btn', 'code-input', 'join-btn', 'submit-btn', etc. Recommend either (a) renaming testIDs in source to match descriptive names from PRD/notes, or (b) updating main agent's documentation/agent-to-agent notes to reflect the actual testIDs. Consider adding explicit testIDs for QR svg ('room-qr'), stats container ('room-stats'), and reports empty state ('reports-empty') for easier future automation.
Optional: address 'shadow*' deprecation warnings by migrating to boxShadow (RN Web).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: clean and concise (~448 lines). MongoDB _id properly excluded everywhere with projection {"_id": 0}. Good use of UUIDs, JWT, bcrypt. Email lowercased on register/login. Match dedup via sorted user pair. No issues found.
Frontend uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL correctly. testIDs present on all critical interactive elements.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed rooms/participants/reports via API. One UI room 'UI_TestRoom' (code SV5DIT) also left active in DB from browser flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend testIDs (actual, verified in /app/frontend/app/**/*.tsx): cta-user, cta-host (landing); email-input, password-input, login-btn, register-btn (host/login); register-submit (host/register); create-room-fab, logout-btn (host/dashboard); name-input, desc-input, create-submit, back-btn (host/create-room); room-code, tab-qr, tab-reports, close-room-btn (host/room); code-input, join-btn, perm-btn (user/join); name-input, age-input, bio-input, photo-camera, photo-gallery, photo-pick, submit-btn (user/profile); like-btn, pass-btn, report-btn, refresh-btn, matches-btn, keep-swiping-btn, goto-chat-btn (user/deck); chat-input, chat-send (user/chat); report-desc, report-submit, report-cancel (report modal); error-text (error). The user deck/swipe/chat UI flow was NOT manually exercised end-to-end through the browser because base64 photo capture requires camera permissions which are unavailable in headless preview; backend already proves the underlying APIs work. If retesting, you can POST /api/participants/join directly to create two users and then drive /user/deck via URL params if the screen supports it.
